title: Multi-Source Market Data Pipeline
summary: Backfills OHLCV bars from Polygon.io and Alpaca IEX into a shared Postgres table, normalizes them, and reports gaps against the real NYSE trading calendar.
problem: Every strategy needs clean, gap-free historical data — pulling it ad hoc from two different APIs each time wastes hours and introduces subtle inconsistencies.

title: Options Chain & Greeks Analyzer
summary: Pulls full options chains from Polygon, computes Greeks via Black-Scholes, and surfaces unusual IV skew across the watchlist.
problem: Raw options chain data is useless without Greeks and skew context — recomputing this by hand per ticker doesn't scale.

title: Dark Pool & Block Trade Tracker
summary: Polls Polygon's dark pool prints, flags block trades well above average volume, and pushes outliers to Telegram.
problem: Large off-exchange prints often precede visible price moves — missing them means reacting late.

title: Econ Calendar & News Sentiment Feed
summary: Fetches this week's high-impact USD econ events and recent headline news, scores each headline with VADER sentiment, and stores both as a queryable feature feed.
problem: The live scanner already reacts to price/volume, but has no signal for macro events or news sentiment shifting the regime.

title: Unusual Options Activity Scanner
summary: Scans options volume/open-interest ratios across the watchlist, flags sweep-like activity, and cross-references it against stock price action.
problem: Unusual options flow is one of the strongest smart-money signals available, but it's invisible without a dedicated scanner watching volume/OI ratios continuously.

title: Sector & Correlation Heatmap
summary: Rolling correlation matrix across a broad ticker universe, rendered as a sector-rotation heatmap on a Flask dashboard.
problem: Sector rotation and correlation regime shifts are easy to miss watching individual tickers one at a time.

title: Real-Time Order Book Depth Visualizer
summary: Streams Level 2 order book data over WebSocket from Alpaca and renders a live depth chart with an imbalance indicator.
problem: Top-of-book price alone hides the supply/demand imbalance that often predicts the next tick.

title: Historical Volatility & IV Rank Database
summary: Nightly job computing realized volatility and IV rank/percentile per ticker, stored for use in strategy screening.
problem: IV rank is central to options strategy selection but requires a rolling historical baseline that most APIs don't provide directly.

title: Insider Trading & 13F Filing Tracker
summary: Scrapes SEC EDGAR for Form 4 and 13F filings, flagging notable insider buys or institutional position changes.
problem: Insider and institutional positioning is public but buried in EDGAR's raw filing format — nobody checks it manually in real time.

title: Macro Dashboard (Fed, CPI, Yields)
summary: Pulls FRED series for rates, CPI, and the yield curve into a single macro regime dashboard.
problem: Equity signals don't exist in a vacuum — a rate-shock or inverted curve regime changes which setups actually work.

title: Vectorized Backtesting Engine
summary: Pandas/NumPy vectorized backtester with realistic slippage and commission modeling, benchmarked against the scanner's built-in backtest.
problem: The scanner's inline backtest engine is convenient but not reusable across other strategies — a standalone engine is needed for anything beyond the live scanner.

title: Walk-Forward Optimization Framework
summary: Rolling train/test window optimizer for strategy parameters, built to catch overfitting that a single in-sample backtest would hide.
problem: Optimizing parameters on the full historical dataset almost always overfits — walk-forward validation is the standard defense.

title: Monte Carlo Strategy Stress Tester
summary: Bootstrap-resamples historical trade sequences to build equity curve confidence bands and a max-drawdown distribution.
problem: A single backtest equity curve is one draw from a distribution of possible outcomes — Monte Carlo resampling shows the real range of risk.

title: Mean-Reversion Pairs Trading Bot
summary: Screens ETF pairs for cointegration (Engle-Granger/Johansen), trades z-score entries/exits, paper-traded through Alpaca.
problem: Directional strategies are exposed to market beta; a cointegrated pairs strategy isolates relative-value edge instead.

title: Momentum Factor Strategy Library
summary: Cross-sectional momentum ranking across a broad ticker universe, rebalancing monthly into the top decile.
problem: Single-ticker signals don't capture cross-sectional edge — ranking a full universe surfaces relative strength the scanner alone can't see.

title: Options Credit Spread Screener & Backtester
summary: Screens for high-probability credit spreads by IV rank and delta, then backtests candidates against historical options data.
problem: Credit spread selection criteria (IV rank, delta, DTE) are easy to state and tedious to screen for by hand across a watchlist every day.

title: Machine-Learning Signal Classifier
summary: Gradient-boosted (XGBoost/LightGBM) classifier predicting next-bar direction from the live scanner's existing signal features.
problem: The scanner's weighted scoring is hand-tuned — an ML classifier trained on the same features tests whether a learned model beats the heuristic.

title: Regime Detection Model (HMM)
summary: Hidden Markov Model classifying trending/ranging/volatile regimes, designed to feed the live scanner's existing regime gate.
problem: The scanner's regime detection today is rule-based (ADX/DMI thresholds); an HMM offers a statistically grounded alternative to validate against.

title: Strategy Performance Attribution Engine
summary: Decomposes realized P&L by signal category (INST/TECH/PATTERN/LEVELS) using the trade journal database from Project #31.
problem: Knowing you're profitable isn't enough — knowing which signal categories actually drive the edge is what makes a strategy improvable.

title: Parameter Sensitivity & Overfitting Auditor
summary: Heatmaps strategy Sharpe/profit-factor across parameter grids and flags knife-edge zones that indicate overfitting.
problem: A great backtest result next to a cliff-edge parameter sensitivity is a red flag that's invisible without explicitly mapping the grid.

title: Smart Order Router
summary: Routes orders to the best available venue/broker based on fees and fill quality, abstracted over multiple broker APIs.
problem: Single-broker execution means accepting whatever fill quality that one venue gives you — routing logic can do better.

title: Order Execution Quality Analyzer
summary: Measures realized slippage against arrival price and VWAP benchmarks across all fills, extending Project #31's fill data.
problem: The trade journal captures slippage per trade, but nobody's aggregated it into a benchmark-relative execution quality score yet.

title: Multi-Broker Abstraction Layer
summary: Unified interface over Alpaca, Interactive Brokers, and Tradier so strategies aren't locked to one broker's SDK.
problem: Every broker integration written directly against Alpaca's SDK has to be rewritten to support a second broker — an abstraction layer avoids that.

title: Redis-Backed Signal Cache & Pub/Sub
summary: Replaces the scanner's global in-memory dicts with Redis, enabling multiple scanner instances to run safely.
problem: The live scanner's shared state (open_trades, dashboard_data) lives in unguarded global dicts — Redis makes that state safe to share across processes.

title: Dockerized Multi-Strategy Orchestrator
summary: Docker Compose stack running independent strategy containers, each with its own isolated config and risk budget.
problem: Running every strategy inside one monolithic process means one bug can take all of them down together.

title: Infrastructure Health & Uptime Monitor
summary: Prometheus + Grafana stack watching container health, API latency, and market-data staleness, alerting to Telegram.
problem: The scanner currently has a /health endpoint but nothing watching it continuously or alerting when it goes stale.

title: Automated Deploy Pipeline for the Scanner
summary: GitHub Actions CI: lint/test, build, then SSH-deploy to EC2 — replacing today's manual git pull + docker compose up.
problem: Every deploy so far has been a manual SSH session; a CI pipeline removes the manual step and its risk of drift.

title: WebSocket Live Price Feed Service
summary: Dedicated Alpaca/Polygon WebSocket consumer publishing normalized ticks to Redis pub/sub for all downstream services.
problem: Every service that needs live prices today polls the REST API separately — one shared WebSocket feed is both cheaper and more consistent.

title: Risk Pre-Trade Checker
summary: Middleware gate running before every submit_alpaca_order() call: hard limits on position size ($5,000), daily realized loss ($500, read from the trade journal), and combined SPY/QQQ/IWM correlated exposure ($10,000). Breaches block the order and fire a Telegram alert; a failure in the checker itself fails closed without ever blocking the scanning loop.
problem: The scanner's position sizing is computed by one code path (_calc_order_qty) with no independent check — a bug there could size or stack positions well past what's safe with nothing to catch it.

title: Disaster Recovery & State Snapshotting
summary: Periodic snapshot of open positions and trade state to S3, with an automated recovery script if the EC2 instance is replaced.
problem: All scanner state today lives on a single EC2 instance's disk — losing that instance would mean losing in-flight trade context with no recovery path.

title: Trade Journal Database
summary: Postgres-backed journal logging every trade the scanner triggers: real Alpaca fills, entry/exit price, P&L, slippage, and outcome, reconciled by a standalone service polling Alpaca's order API.
problem: Trade outcomes were previously simulated by polling price against a calculated stop/TP, capped at the last 500 entries in a flat JSON file — never the broker's real fill data, and never durable.

title: Portfolio VaR & Drawdown Monitor
summary: Computes historical and parametric Value-at-Risk plus live drawdown from the trade journal, alerting as limits are approached.
problem: Per-trade risk is capped by Project #29, but nothing today tracks portfolio-level VaR or cumulative drawdown across all open and closed trades.

title: Kelly/Vol-Target Position Sizing Optimizer
summary: Dynamic position sizing combining fractional Kelly and volatility targeting, to replace the scanner's fixed ATR-risk sizing.
problem: Fixed 1%-of-account risk per trade doesn't account for a strategy's actual edge or the current volatility regime the way Kelly/vol-targeting does.

title: Correlated Exposure Dashboard
summary: Visualizes net exposure across correlated tickers (SPY/QQQ/IWM) in real time, feeding Project #29's correlation limit.
problem: Project #29 enforces a correlated exposure cap, but there's no dashboard to see that exposure building up before the limit actually blocks a trade.

title: Daily P&L & Risk Report Generator
summary: Auto-generates an end-of-day email/PDF summary from the trade journal: P&L, win rate, R-multiple distribution, and worst trade.
problem: Reviewing the day's trading currently means manually querying the trade journal — a daily report makes that a passive habit instead of a chore.

title: Strategy Capital Allocation Engine
summary: Allocates capital across multiple strategies/tickers based on rolling Sharpe ratio and correlation, rebalanced weekly.
problem: Running multiple strategies with fixed, hand-picked capital splits ignores which ones are actually earning their allocation right now.

title: Stress-Test & Scenario Simulator
summary: Replays historical crash days (2020 COVID crash, 2022 rate shock) against current positions to estimate hypothetical loss.
problem: A backtest over normal market conditions says nothing about how the current portfolio would survive a genuine tail event.

title: Kill-Switch & Circuit Breaker Service
summary: Independent watchdog that force-flattens all positions and halts trading if daily loss or error rate exceeds a hard threshold.
problem: Project #29 blocks new orders past the daily loss limit, but nothing yet actively flattens existing open positions if things go badly wrong fast.

title: Portfolio Rebalancing Scheduler
summary: Periodic rebalancing job keeping strategy capital allocations within target bands, logging every rebalance trade to the journal.
problem: Capital allocations drift as strategies win and lose — without a scheduler, rebalancing only happens when someone remembers to do it.

title: Compliance & Audit Trail Logger
summary: Immutable, append-only log of every risk-gate decision (pass/block) and config change, for post-hoc audit of trading decisions.
problem: Risk gates like Project #29 print to logs today, but there's no durable, tamper-evident audit trail of every decision they made.

title: LLM Trade Journal Analyst Agent
summary: Claude-powered agent that reads the trade journal nightly and writes a plain-English "what worked / what didn't" summary.
problem: The trade journal has all the data needed to explain the day's performance, but reading raw rows isn't the same as getting a clear narrative.

title: Natural-Language Strategy Query Bot
summary: Telegram bot backed by an LLM that answers questions like "how did SPY trades do this week?" by querying the trade journal directly.
problem: Ad hoc questions about performance currently require writing SQL by hand — a chat interface makes the journal accessible on the go.

title: Autonomous Backtest-and-Report Agent
summary: Agent that takes a proposed strategy tweak, runs it through Project #11's backtester, and produces a comparison report against the current baseline.
problem: Testing a strategy idea today means manually wiring up a backtest run each time — an agent can do that round-trip on request.

title: RAG Pipeline over SEC Filings & Earnings Calls
summary: Vector database (pgvector) over 10-K/10-Q filings and earnings call transcripts, with semantic search and LLM summarization for fundamental context.
problem: Technical signals say nothing about fundamentals — a RAG pipeline over filings adds that context without manually reading every 10-K.

title: Signal Explainability Agent
summary: Given any fired signal, an LLM agent explains in plain English which factors (INST/TECH/PATTERN) actually drove the score, using the signal log.
problem: The scanner's weighted score is a number — understanding *why* a signal fired at 62% conviction instead of 30% currently takes manually reading the signal log.

title: Anomaly Detection Agent for Regime Shifts
summary: LLM + statistical agent monitoring live data for regime shifts, proactively messaging a Telegram alert with its reasoning.
problem: Regime shifts (trending to choppy, low-vol to high-vol) change which strategies work — catching the shift early, with a reason attached, beats reacting after the fact.

title: Multi-Agent Strategy Research Team
summary: A small crew of LLM agents (data researcher, backtester, critic) that collaboratively propose and stress-test new signal ideas.
problem: Coming up with, backtesting, and critiquing a new signal idea end-to-end is slow doing it alone — a multi-agent workflow can parallelize the research loop.

title: Automated Code Review Agent for Strategy Changes
summary: Hooks into Project #27's CI pipeline to have an LLM review every diff to live_spx_scanner.py for risk-relevant changes before merge.
problem: Changes to the live trading file today get reviewed by whoever's making them — an automated second reviewer focused specifically on risk-relevant changes adds a safety net.

title: Voice-Briefed Morning Market Prep Agent
summary: Generates and reads aloud (TTS) a pre-market briefing combining the econ calendar, overnight gaps, and current open risk exposure.
problem: Pre-market prep means checking several separate sources by hand every morning — a single spoken briefing consolidates it into one habit.

title: Self-Tuning Parameter Agent
summary: LLM-guided agent that reviews Project #20's sensitivity analysis and proposes (never auto-applies) parameter adjustments with reasoning, for human approval.
problem: Parameter tuning based on sensitivity analysis is valuable but easy to neglect — an agent that proposes changes with reasoning keeps a human in the loop without doing the grunt work.
